intro: Sift is paid subscription; SEON is custom-priced. Sift: Digital trust and safety platform using ML to score account, payment, and content abuse across the customer lifecycle. SEON: Fraud & Risk Management tool for developers. Specializes in fraud detection. Sift fits marketplace — in practice, high-value transactions. SEON fits fintech, e-commerce, and marketplace platforms. On our rubric Sift scores 4/5 for developer experience and 3/5 for transparency, while SEON scores 4/5 and 1/5. The honest trade-off: Sift's main drawback — Low transaction volume; SEON's — Overly aggressive rules can block legitimate users. For SEON: ML-based detection catches fraud patterns in real time. Sift is explicitly not the right pick for low transaction volume. SEON is not aimed at internal tools or low-risk B2B SaaS. Another Sift advantage: Marketplace with seller risk. Another Sift friction point: B2B with trusted counterparties. Our read on Sift: best for high-value transactions. Sift offers subscription from $4.2. On SEON: SEON is a Fraud & Risk Management tool.
quick_take: Sift is for marketplace; SEON is for fintech, e-commerce, and marketplace platforms; decide on pricing model.
verdict_a: Choose Sift if your work looks like high-value transactions, and if transparency (3/5 vs 1/5 in our rubric) matters to your selection.
verdict_b: Choose SEON if fintech, e-commerce, and marketplace platforms, and if you're buying at enterprise scale where custom terms apply.

faq.a: Sift uses paid subscription pricing; SEON uses custom-priced. Which is cheaper depends on your volume and team size — model both against your projected usage before deciding.

faq.q: Can I migrate from Sift to SEON?
faq.a: Expect real effort: Sift is medium lock-in (ML scoring API, integration moderate) and SEON is medium lock-in (API-based fraud scoring). Migrating between them means rebuilding integrations, re-authoring config, and accepting new coupling. Scope a spike before committing.

faq.q: Which has better developer experience, Sift or SEON?
faq.a: Both score 4/5 for developer experience in our rubric, so neither has a structural edge. The practical answer depends on stack fit: Sift's ergonomics suit some workflows, SEON's suit others. Try both on a throwaway project before committing.

faq.q: Is SEON a good alternative to Sift?
faq.a: They sit in the same category, so yes — SEON is a plausible alternative for many Sift use cases. It fits best when fintech, e-commerce, and marketplace platforms. Skip it if internal tools or low-risk B2B SaaS.
